content:
Member-only story

THE COST OF AI TOOLS
GitHub Copilot went from charging per prompt to per token, and the bill turned out to be about me

14 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Real Cost of an AI Coding Assistant (Illustration generated with Google Gemini)
Something genuinely new happened in the last year. We can now describe what we want in plain English, and new software comes out the other end. People are calling it vibe coding or AI assisted coding, and the name fits - you bring the idea and the vibe, the coding assistant brings the syntax. A person who never learned to code can now build the small app they always wanted. A designer can demo a working application instead of handing off a mockup or a wireframe UI. A founder can stand up a fully working prototype over a weekend. Everyone is a developer now, at least a little, and it feels superhuman. I felt it too. For about a year I walked around with the quiet sense that I had grown a second pair of hands.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Vibe coding: everyone’s a developer now (Illustration generated with Google Gemini)
And then the bill came, and the superhuman feeling turned out to have a price I hadn’t been looking at. At work, the credits are limited, its rationed and provided to each user with a limit of usage. Earlier, the billing was fixed and based on the model that we…
